discoveredIssues.summary: Live-stack e2e for spec 075 (TP-075-08 e2e, TP-075-09, TP-075-15, TP-075-16) blocked because smackerel-test-smackerel-core-1 exits with [F061-SCENARIO-MISSING]: config/prompt_contracts/retrieval-qa-v1.yaml allowed_tools[1]=entity_resolve and config/prompt_contracts/weather-query-v1.yaml allowed_tools[0]=location_normalize are not registered in the runtime tool registry; loader rejects → assistant/scenarios.yaml referential-integrity check fails → core fatal-exits before any e2e test runs. Foreign-owned by spec 061 (scenario loader / tool registry).
discoveredIssues.closureNote: Root cause resolved: entity_resolve and location_normalize micro-tools are registered in the agent registry via init() in internal/agent/tools/microtools/entity_resolve.go and internal/agent/tools/microtools/location_normalize*.go (EntityResolveToolName / LocationNormalizeToolName). Scenario loader no longer rejects retrieval-qa-v1.yaml or weather-query-v1.yaml; smackerel-test-smackerel-core-1 reaches healthy. Spec 075 substitute-evidence certification (integration-lane PASS) stands; the live e2e lane is now unblocked for future runs without altering the certified close-out.

executionHistory.summary: Bootstrapped the rollout safety net for spec 066 legacy retirement: per-user-per-command one-time deprecation notice, residual-usage telemetry with rolling 7-day report, automatic pause on SST-defined rollback thresholds, post-window observation gate before final handler deletion. 11 BDD scenarios (SCN-075-A01..A11) cover first-invocation NL-served notice, no re-notification, per-command independence, telemetry counters, rollback-threshold auto-pause, resume-resets-counter, window-closed canonical response, post-window zero-invocation proof, cross-transport dedup persistence, fail-loud SST, and PII-free telemetry.

Created design.md for legacy retirement telemetry and user communications, resolving the notice ledger, runtime pause state, HMAC user buckets, post-window observation report, fail-loud SST keys, dashboard panels, and scenario-to-test mapping for SCN-075-A01..A11.

Created planning artifacts for SCN-075-A01..A11: five sequential scopes, scenario-manifest.json, test-plan.json, uservalidation.md, and planning-only report.md. Status remains in_progress with planMaturityOnly=true.

Mode metadata reconciliation only. spec.md declares Workflow Mode: full-delivery (authoritative owner intent set 2026-05-31); prior bubbles.plan run defensively wrote workflowMode=product-to-planning, statusCeiling=specs_hardened, planningOnly=true while operating under a planning-only workflow. Reconciled state.json to workflowMode=full-delivery, statusCeiling=done, planningOnly=false; preserved planMaturityOnly=true because planning artifacts are complete and no implementation evidence exists yet. certification.status remains in_progress; no implementation, test, or certification claims made.

Decomposed the previously route_required 'SCOPE-075-06 facade rollout' work item into Scope 6 with five sequential sub-scopes (6.1 facade Policy dispatch contract + 5-branch unit test, 6.2 cmd/core/wiring_assistant_facade.go construction wiring, 6.3 PWA renderer + live Playwright TP-075-09, 6.4 WhatsApp+Mobile renderers + Telegram interceptor short-circuit, 6.5 live-stack execution of TP-075-05/06/07/08, TP-075-13/14/15, TP-075-16/17). Added scenarios SCN-075-A12 (facade five-branch dispatch) and SCN-075-A13 (Telegram interceptor short-circuit). Added tests TP-075-19, TP-075-20, TP-075-21, TP-075-22, TP-075-23, TP-075-24 and re-targeted TP-075-09 execution to SCOPE-075-06.3. Recorded a Telegram Coexistence Decision Record recommending option 2 (interceptor short-circuits via assistantFacadeUpstream context key when facade is upstream); decision MUST be ratified by bubbles.design in design.md before Scope 6.4 implementation begins. Status remains in_progress; planMaturityOnly stays true; no implementation, test, or certification evidence produced.

SCOPE-075-06.2: facade construction wiring. cmd/core/wiring_assistant_facade.go now constructs the spec 075 legacy-retirement Policy via NewMultiResidualTelemetry(prom, sql) (catalog from SST notice/unknown copy, SQL notice ledger, SST window-state resolver with static not-paused reader, HMAC user bucket hasher, NewPrometheusResidualTelemetry + NewSQLResidualStore fanned out via NewMultiResidualTelemetry) and threads the Policy through FacadeConfig.Policy before NewFacade. Added cmd/core/wiring_assistant_facade_test.go with TP-075-20: 8 sub-tests covering the happy path and every fail-loud branch (nil config, nil pool, nil clock, empty WindowID, empty HMAC key, empty notice copy, invalid window state). go test ./cmd/core/ passes (0.761s). DoD 6.2 checked. No transport or facade code modified.

Covers internal/assistant/schema/assistant_turn_v1.json + NoticePayload sub-def, internal/assistant/schema/types.go TurnResponse.Notice, golden fixture internal/assistant/schema/testdata/response_v1.json (notice-present + notice-absent), internal/assistant/httpadapter/{schema.go,adapter.go RenderJSON,middleware.go (if needed)}, web/pwa/generated/* regen via cmd/web-assistant-codegen, and Flutter shared-core regen under clients/mobile/assistant/lib/shared_core/generated/*. Field is OPTIONAL/additive so v1-compatible — schema_version stays 'v1', no bump needed; decision documented in scopes.md and routed to bubbles.design to record under design.md. Sub-scope inventory + test-plan + scenario-manifest + uservalidation + currentScope updated. No source/tests/docs touched. Returning route_required to dispatch bubbles.design (v1-compat decision record in design.md) and bubbles.implement (SCOPE-075-06.2b execution).

SCOPE-075-06.2b implementation. Added optional top-level `notice` property + `NoticePayload` sub-def to internal/assistant/schema/assistant_turn_v1.json (additive, v1-compatible, schema_version unchanged). Added TurnResponse.Notice + NoticePayload to internal/assistant/schema/types.go (omitempty). Relaxed internal/assistant/schema/golden_contract_test.go helpers so `properties ⊇ required` is allowed with `,omitempty` enforced on Go optionals; added the NoticePayload pinning subtest and a notice-present round-trip subtest; adversarial drift checks preserved. Added TurnResponse.Notice *NoticeJSON (omitempty) + NoticeJSON to internal/assistant/httpadapter/schema.go. Wired internal/assistant/httpadapter/adapter.go::RenderJSON to copy AssistantResponse.LegacyRetirementNotice -> TurnResponse.Notice nil-safely. Extended internal/assistant/schema/webcodegen/generator.go (added NoticePayload to definitionOrder; optional-field iteration; JS validator wraps optional checks in hasOwnProperty && !== null; DTS emits `field?: Type`). Extended clients/mobile/assistant/lib/src/codegen.dart with the same optional-field iteration and a containsKey && != null wrapper. Regenerated web/pwa/generated/assistant_turn_v1.{js,d.ts} via `go run ./cmd/web-assistant-codegen` and clients/mobile/assistant/lib/core/generated/assistant_turn_v1.dart via `dart run tool/gen_dart_models.dart`. TP-075-25 (schema + httpadapter golden tests), TP-075-26 (web/pwa/tests drift test), TP-075-27 (flutter test codegen_drift_test.dart) all PASS. Regression sweep `go test ./internal/assistant/... ./web/pwa/tests/` clean. Containment preserved: no renderer code, no schema_version change, no other TurnResponse field touched. design.md v1-compat decision record is foreign-owned (bubbles.design) and is NOT updated by this agent. Routing bubbles.design follow-up for the design.md record.

SCOPE-075-06.3 implementation. Extended web/pwa/lib/render_descriptor_v1.js to project the OPTIONAL TurnResponse.notice payload as a `kind:"text"` node appended AFTER the primary body (one-line non-blocking addendum, never replaces the body). The branch is dormant when `notice` is absent so the spec 073 cross-language renderer canary (Dart vs JS) stays green against the existing 7 fixtures under tests/fixtures/assistant_response_v1/ (none of which carry notice). Confirmed: `go test -run TestRenderDescriptorV1_CrossLanguageCanary ./tests/unit/clients/` -> ok 6.273s. Added Go e2e at tests/e2e/assistant/legacy_retirement_notice_test.go (re-targeted TP-075-09, patterned after tests/e2e/photos_capability_test.go) with two cases: (1) TestLegacyRetirementNoticeE2E_OpenWindowRendersAddendumWithoutBlockingBody posts retired command `/weather` during the open window, asserts response.notice.{command,replacement_example,copy_key,window_id} populated, asserts body non-empty (notice MUST NOT replace body), then runs the PWA JS renderer subprocess and asserts the descriptor contains body as the FIRST text node and replacement_example as a LATER text node; (2) TestLegacyRetirementNoticeE2E_NonRetiredTurnOmitsNotice is adversarial: a benign `hello` turn MUST omit the notice key (omitempty back-compat) and the renderer descriptor MUST be byte-stable against a re-run with notice stripped (catches phantom-notice regression). `go vet -tags=e2e ./tests/e2e/assistant/` clean. DoD checkbox for SCOPE-075-06.3 stays unchecked: live-stack PASS evidence requires a running stack with CORE_EXTERNAL_URL + SMACKEREL_AUTH_TOKEN + LEGACY_RETIREMENT_WINDOW_STATE=open + LEGACY_RETIREMENT_WINDOW_ID exported by the e2e harness, which was not available in this session. The renderer + e2e artifacts shipped here are the prerequisites for the SCOPE-075-06.5 live-stack execution row. No other scope files, schema, generated bindings, transport renderers, telegram interceptor, or facade code were touched.
